title: Monotoca elliptica
intro: Monotoca elliptica, also known as the elliptical baekea or baekea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae, native to eastern Australia. The health benefits of Monotoca elliptica include its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may be beneficial in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of this plant include monoterpene glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Monotoca elliptica is often prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is used to treat a range of ailments, including coughs, colds, and skin irritations.

This family is a part of the order Ericales, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The order Ericales is further grouped into the clade Eurosids I, a larger clade of flowering plants.
